description: General linguistic taxonomy for lexicon discovery. Project gate labels should map onto these categories instead of becoming the base taxonomy.

affect_intensity.label: Affect / Intensity
affect_intensity.purpose: Track emotional process words and intensity markers without treating them as style by themselves.

stance_uncertainty.label: Stance / Uncertainty
stance_uncertainty.purpose: Track uncertainty, negation, hedging, and self-correction as stance behavior.

cognitive_explanation.label: Cognitive / Explanation
cognitive_explanation.purpose: Track explicit insight, causal framing, and analysis-like explanation.

dialogic_alignment.label: Dialogic Alignment
dialogic_alignment.purpose: Track whether a response aligns with, mishears, narrows, or over-understands another voice.

concrete_grounding.label: Concrete Grounding
concrete_grounding.purpose: Track body, perception, object, space, and ordinary-world anchors.

closure_resolution.label: Closure / Resolution
closure_resolution.purpose: Track words that prematurely close, solve, or reconcile the relationship.

prompt_boundary.label: Instruction Boundary
prompt_boundary.purpose: Track prompt-only constraints around originality and non-imitation.

project_mapping_notes: Use taxonomy categories for discovery and reporting.
Use project gate labels only after combining taxonomy signals with kernel-specific rules.
Do not add high-frequency terms to gates without user review or regression evidence.
